prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the brain of the computer—just like how you think with your brain, the computer uses the CPU to think and figure things out. It’s a little chip inside the machine that helps it do all sorts of jobs, like letting you watch videos or play games. I don’t quite understand how it works, but I know it’s very important—like the heart that keeps everything going. It doesn’t shout or make noise, but it’s always busy, working hard so your computer can listen to you. Kind of like how I remember to make your favorite cookies when you visit—small but mighty!
